title: Governor, National Bank of Cambodia  |  First female central bank governor in Cambodia's history  |  Permanent Vice-Chair, National Coordination Committee on AML/CFT

leader.name: Chea Serey
leader.purpose: Mental model schema mapping decision-making architecture across 7 dimensions Born 1981, Phnom Penh. Commerce degree (Victoria University, NZ). PhD Economics — financial development and inclusion in ASEAN LDCs (University of Adelaide, 2021). Joined NBC banking supervision 2002. Director-General 2013. Governor July 2023. Daughter of predecessor Chea Chanto (governor 1994–2023). Rated A- by Global Finance 2024 and 2025.

growth_theory.assessed_position: Serey's growth theory is structurally distinct from all other profiles: she focuses on the financial system as an independent growth lever. Her PhD dissertation examined how financial development and inclusion reduce poverty and support growth in ASEAN LDCs. growth_theory.primary_quote: Maintaining the riel's stability has contributed to price stability in the market. In 2023, the riel depreciated only about 0.9%, even as other regional currencies saw much sharper losses.
subfields: Core Thesis: Monetary sovereignty and financial inclusion as prerequisites for growth agency

risk_tolerance.assessed_position: Serey has taken genuine institutional risks: launching Bakong as a blockchain-based retail payment system (first of its kind in the region); piloting CBDC-adjacent cross-border QR with Japan, Singapore, India; pushing de-dollarisation against entrenched banking sector resistance. She cracked down on fraudulent microcredit institutions (50 licences terminated H1 2018 alone) despite industry pushbac
subfields: Assessment: MEDIUM — Innovative on instruments; conservative on systemic risk

time_horizon.assessed_position: De-dollarisation is generationally long — she acknowledges it will take decades to shift Cambodian behaviour from USD to riel. Her Bakong cross-border payment links (Japan Phase 1 2025, Singapore/India 2025, BIS NEXUS entry 2025) are 10-year infrastructure investments. Her school curriculum integration of financial literacy is a 20-year human capital project. She is simultaneously managing near-te
subfields: Revealed: 10–20 years (de-dollarisation)  |  Near: 2025 (NPL management, credit slowdown)

dependency_assumptions.assessed_position: 1. Riel adoption is voluntary behaviour change, not just regulatory mandate: NBC can require banks to hold 10% of loans in riel and require government pricing in riel, but actual riel adoption depends on public trust that will not be legislated. 2. subfields: The monetary transition model's load-bearing assumptions

institution_vs_relationship.assessed_position: Serey is the most strongly institution-oriented official in the sample. She built the Credit Bureau Cambodia (Cambodia's first), the Bakong payment system, and NBC's Financial Stability Department. Her PhD on financial inclusion reflects intellectual institutional commitment, not merely positional. The significant caveat: she directly succeeded her father as NBC Governor, which raises legitimate q
subfields: Verdict: Strongly institutional — with a significant legacy question

global_positioning_logic.assessed_position: Serey's global positioning is financial sovereignty through regional infrastructure. Cross-border QR links with Thailand, Laos, Vietnam, Malaysia, South Korea, Japan, and through Alipay+ with 10+ international wallets — all settled in riel — reduce Cambodia's dependence on dollar-intermediated settlement and give NBC more transaction-level monetary data. subfields: Theory: Regional payment architecture as monetary sovereignty infrastructure

Overall: HIGHEST consistency score alongside Sok Siphana. Her stated reasoning (monetary sovereignty through riel promotion and digital infrastructure) reliably predicts her actions. Institutional integrity question on the appointment process is the only structural caveat.
